title: Gear selector repair [SOLVED]
description: Mercedes-Benz diagnostic trouble code P1856: Gear selector repair [SOLVED]
probable_causes: Gear selector repair [SOLVED]
Wiring, connector, or sensor/actuator fault in the related circuit
Component wear or contamination common on higher-mileage vehicles
symptoms: Check engine light (MIL) illuminated
Possible limp mode or reduced performance depending on fault
Related system performance fault flags in scan data
step_by_step_fix: Confirm P1856 with a scan tool; note freeze-frame and readiness monitors.
Inspect related wiring harness, connectors, and grounds for corrosion or damage.
Check transmission fluid level and condition.
Inspect speed sensor wiring and transmission control module connections.
Consult transmission-specific repair data before internal repairs.
safety_warning: Disconnect the battery when working on airbag or SRS-related circuits. Use proper jack stands and allow the engine to cool before exhaust or cooling system work.

title: Heated Oxygen Sensor (HO2S) Circuit High Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 2
description: Mercedes-Benz diagnostic trouble code P0138: Heated Oxygen Sensor (HO2S) Circuit High Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 2
probable_causes: Heated Oxygen Sensor (HO2S) Circuit High Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 2
Wiring, connector, or sensor/actuator fault in the related circuit
Component wear or contamination common on higher-mileage vehicles
symptoms: Check engine light (MIL) illuminated
Possible limp mode or reduced performance depending on fault
Related system performance fault flags in scan data
step_by_step_fix: Confirm P0138 with a scan tool; note freeze-frame and readiness monitors.
Inspect related wiring harness, connectors, and grounds for corrosion or damage.
Check sensor heater fuse and voltage supply at the sensor connector.
Measure sensor signal and heater resistance against specification.
Replace the affected O2 sensor if out of spec; clear codes and verify closed-loop fuel control.
safety_warning: Disconnect the battery when working on airbag or SRS-related circuits. Use proper jack stands and allow the engine to cool before exhaust or cooling system work.
